title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
description: Allbirds is ditching wool sneakers for AI servers, rebranding as NewBird AI after locking in a $50M convertible financing facility.

title: What’s the deal with Alzheimer’s disease and amyloid?
description: For decades, scientists have concentrated on what now looks to be a blind alley.

title: Blue Origin has a new employee stock plan, but not everyone is happy
description: "We are being intentional about creating liquidity events."

title: It's Tax Day, and no one knows how to file for prediction market winnings
description: It's time for Americans to pay taxes on prediction market winnings, but no one knows how.

title: Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
description: Reid Hoffman says tracking AI token use can gauge adoption, but cautions it should be paired with context and not treated as a direct productivity metric.

title: Adobe’s new Firefly AI assistant can use Creative Cloud apps to complete tasks
description: Adobe says the assistant can work across apps like Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express,  Illustrator and its other apps to do tasks for you.

title: Gitar, a startup that uses agents to secure code, emerges from stealth with $9 million
description: The company uses AI to review code that, more often than not these days, has also been generated by AI.

title: This startup is betting tokenmaxxing will create the next compute giant
description: Parasail raised $32 million in a Series A, signaling a fractured future of models and compute.

title: The Download: NASA’s nuclear spacecraft and unveiling our AI 10

title: Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit tools sold on Telegram
description: From inside a money-laundering center in Cambodia, an employee opens a popular Vietnamese banking app on his phone. title: AI Slop Is Making the Internet Fake-Happy
description: A new study examines the impact of the rise of AI-generated websites on the internet—and found some surprising results.

title: The Deepfake Nudes Crisis in Schools Is Much Worse Than You Thought
description: An analysis by WIRED and Indicator found nearly 90 schools and 600 students around the world impacted by AI-generated deepfake nude images—and the problem shows no signs of going away.

title: No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all
description: For four days in February 2019, some 30 synthetic biologists and ethicists hunkered down at a conference center in Northern Virginia to brainstorm high-risk, cutting-­edge, irresistibly exciting ideas that the National Science Foundation should fund. title: Building trust in the AI era with privacy-led UX
description: The practice of privacy-led user experience (UX) is a design philosophy that treats transparency around data collection and usage as an integral part of the customer relationship. title: Ukraine’s military robot surge aims to offset drone risks to humans
description: Ukraine is replacing more soldiers with robots in the battlefield kill zone.

title: Sony killing features for antenna, set-top box users of Bravia smart TVs in May
description: Some 2023 and 2024 models are also affected.

title: Redefining the future of software engineering
description: Software engineering has experienced two seismic shifts this century. First was the rise of the open source movement, which gradually made code accessible to developers and engineers everywhere. title: How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
description: The premade Skills available through the Gemini sidebar in Chrome include ways to maximize protein in recipes or summarize YouTube videos.

title: Anthropic Opposes the Extreme AI Liability Bill That OpenAI Backed
description: Anthropic and OpenAI are clashing over a proposed Illinois law that would let AI labs largely off the hook for mass deaths and financial disasters.

title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own
description: Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee, helped pass one of the country’s toughest AI laws. Now Silicon Valley’s biggest names are trying to stop his rise to Congress.

description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
